CheckTitle: Detect users with multiple high-privilege role assignments
Description: Checks whether any user is assigned two or more high-privilege directory roles simultaneously. Role accumulation violates the principle of least privilege and increases blast radius if the account is compromised.
Risk: A user holding multiple high-privilege roles (e.g., Global Admin + Exchange Admin) has an unnecessarily large blast radius. If the account is compromised, the attacker gains the combined privileges of all assigned roles. This also indicates a lack of role separation.

Remediation.Recommendation: Review users with multiple high-privilege roles and remove unnecessary assignments. Use separate accounts for different administrative functions. Implement PIM for just-in-time activation of roles.

SourceNotes: Separation of duties is a fundamental security principle.
